sous_titres.text: Will you take a look at that?
but that llama you're looking at was once a human being.
And not just any human being.
A rich, powerful ball of charisma.
I was the world's nicest guy, and they ruined my life for no reason.
Oh, is that hard to believe?
Look, I tell you what. You go back aways...
You know, before I was a llama, and this will all make sense.
All right, now see, that's a little too far back.
There are blue bloods with the intellects of fleas
There are kings and catty tyrants
Who are so lacking in refinements
They'd be better suited swinging from the trees
He was born and raised to rule
No one has ever been as cool
In a thousand years of aristocracy
The quintessence of perfection that is he
Okay, this is the real me. Not this.
Okay, see this palace? Everyone in it is at my command.
He's the sovereign lord of the nation
He's the hippest cat in creation
He's the alpha, the omega, A to Z
And this perfect world will spin
'Cause this perfect world begins and ends with
He's the king of the world
I'm sorry, but you've thrown off the emperor's groove.
Don't you know he's the king of the world?
Aah! Your Highness, it is time for you to choose your bride.
And let me guess. You have a great personality.
Is this really the best you could do?
Oh, yes. Oh, no. I mean, perhaps...
He's like the thing that wouldn't shut up.
Anyway, still wondering about that llama in the opening?
Well, let me show you the people responsible for ruining my life.
Uh, excuse me. I'm here to see Emperor Kuzco.
You see, I got this summons...
Inside, up the stairs, and to the left. Just follow the signs.
Uh, and don't be fooled by the folksy peasant look.
Aah! Oh, hey. Are you all right? Here. Let me...
- Oh, you're so very kind. - What happened?
Well, I threw off the emperor's groove.
The rhythm in which he lives his life, his pattern of behavior.
and the emperor had me thrown out the window.
Oh, really? I'm supposed to see him today.
Hey, are you gonna be all right?
This guy's trouble, but as bad as he is,
he is nothing compared to what's coming up next.
And why have you come here today?
Well... Your Highness... I mean, Your Grace.
Okay, gang. Check out this piece of work.
This is Yzma, the emperor's advisor,
living proof that dinosaurs once roamed the Earth.
And let's not forget Yzma's right-hand man.
Every decade or so she gets a new one.
This year's model is called Kronk.
Yeah, I got that there, Yzma.
Now lately, Yzma's gotten into this bad habit
of trying to run the country behind my back,
and I'm thinkin' that's got to stop.
It is no concern of mine whether your family has...
Ha! You really should have thought of that before you became peasants.
We're through here. Take him away. Next!
The nerve of some of those peasants, huh?
Uh, you were doing it again.
I'm the emperor, and you're the emperor's advisor.
I was only dealing with meaningless peasant matters.
What is holding this woman together?
What the... How long has that been there?
Good thinkin', Yzma. What do you say, Kuzco?
Uh, excuse me, Your Highness. The village leader is here to see you.
Oh, and by the way, you're fired.
Um, how else can I say it?
You're being let go, your department's being downsized,
you're part of an outplacement, we're going in a different direction,
we're not picking up your option... Take your pick. I got more.
I have been nothing if not loyal to the empire
Hey, hey, everybody hits their stride.
You just hit yours 50 years ago.
Very good, Kronk. Here. Get the snack.
Okay, you heard the man. Up, up, up.
I'm here because I received a summons...
Hey, there he is! My main village man.
That's right. You are just the man I wanted to see.
Word on the street is you can fix my problem.
You can fix my problem, can't you?
Sure. I'll do what I can.
Good, good. That's just what I wanted to hear.
Are you aware of just how important your village is to the empire?
Well, I know we grow the crops that you use here at the palace.
We also herd the llamas that you...
You got a pretty sweet little setup there
on top of that hill, don't you?
Yeah. My family has lived on that hilltop for the last six generations.
So tell me, where do you find you get the most sun?
Oh, I'd say just on the other side of those trees.
When the sun hits that ridge just right, these hills sing.
That's it? That's all you wanted me for?
I just needed an insider's opinion before I okay'd this spot for my pool.
my ultimate summer getaway complete with water slide.
It's my birthday gift to me. Ha!
Um... I don't understand how this could happen.
Well, let me clear it up for you.
At my birthday celebration tomorrow, I give the word,
and your town will be destroyed
So, if I were you, I'd pick up some change-of-address forms
But, um, where will we live?
Don't know, don't care. How's that?
your little town thingy will be bye-bye.
Oh, yeah. Everything was goin' my way.
He can't get rid of me that easily.
Who does that ungrateful little worm think he is?
Does he... A little to the left.
Have any idea of who he's dealing with?
How could he do this to me? Why, I practically raised him.
Yeah, you think he would've turned out better.
Well, it's better you're takin' out your anger
on these things instead of the real Kuzco, huh?
With him out of the way and no heir to the throne,
I'll take over and rule the empire.
So how does that work with you bein' fired and all?
The only ones who know about that are the three of us,
soon to be the two of us.
And I'm one of those two, right?
Why do we even have that lever?
and keep your arms and legs in at all times.
Yzma, put your hands in the air!
Ah, how shall I do it?
I'll turn him into a flea,
and then I'll put that flea in a box,
and then I'll put that box inside of another box,
and then I'll mail that box to myself,
I'll smash it with a hammer!
It's brilliant, brilliant, brilliant, I tell you!
I'll just poison him with this.
Take it, Kronk. Oh, ho, ho.
So, is everything ready for tonight?
Oh, yeah. I thought we'd start off with soup and a light salad
and then see how we feel after that.
the poison chosen specially to kill Kuzco,
A few drops in his drink, then I'll propose a toast,
and he will be dead before dessert.
Which is a real shame, because it's gonna be delicious.
I am one hungry king of the world.
So, no hard feelings about being let go?
Kronk, get the emperor a drink.
Kronk. The emperor needs his drink.
Hey, Kronky, everything okay back there?
Ooh. The drinks were a bit on the...
Hey, did you see that sky today? Talk about blue.
Oh, they're so easy to make. I'll get you the recipe.
Now to get rid of the body.
Uh, we were just making a toast
Right. So what are you gonna do?
I mean, you've been around here a long time,
and I really mean a long time.
It might be difficult for someone of your age
adjusting to life in the private sector.
Hey, Kronk, can you top me off, pal? Be a friend? Ha, ha.
Now, about you finding new work...
Let's face it. You're no spring chicken,
and I mean that in the best possible way.
This is extract of llama. Ugh!
You know, in my defense, your poisons all look alike.
You might think about relabeling some of them.
Take him out of town and finish the job now!
Kronk, this is kind of important.
Well, I suppose there's time for dessert.
All right. A quick cup of coffee.
Then take him out of town and finish the job!
Guess where I am right now.
Still think I'm not the victim here? Watch. It gets better.
Ugh, he's doing his own theme music?
I am so glad I was unconscious for all of this.
You're not just gonna let him die like that, are you?
He's trying to lead you down the path of righteousness.
I'm gonna lead you down the path that rocks.
- Oh, come off it. - You come off it!
I got three good reasons why you should just walk away.
Number one... Look at that guy!
He's got that sissy stringy music thing.
We've been through this. It's a harp, and you know it.
But, what does that have to do with anything?
No, no. He's got a point.
You're sort of confusing me, so begone!
Uh, or, uh, you know. However I get rid of you guys.
Um, what's with the chimp and the bug?
Can we get back to me?
What to do, what to do? What do we do with the body?
What am I gonna tell the village?
Come on, Kronky. Come on, Kronky. Okay.
What do I do? What do I do?
Oh, hey! Hey, you! Hey! Excuse me. Excuse me.
Stop! Pardon me. Excuse me. Sorry about that.
Comin' through. Hey, you with the cart!
Uhh. Hope that doesn't come back to haunt me.
Mom, Mom! I think I'm still growing!
All right, Tipo. Stand still and let's see.
Mom, you and I both know that it's impossible for him
- to have grown in the last five minutes. - Mmm! Mmm!
What? Tipo, get out of the way. It's my turn again. Measure me.
Dad! I ate a bug today!
Heh. Don't tell her I said that.
Lady with a baby comin' through.
Dad, Dad, Dad! Look at how big I am!
I'm going through a growth spurt.
I'm as big as you were when you were me.
That's not as impressive as my loose tooth. See?
Our deal was that you could stay awake until Daddy came home.
No, you two can stay up.
We're just gonna be sittin' here tellin' each other
how much we love each other.
So what did the emperor want?
You know what? He couldn't see me.
- Couldn't see you? Why not? - I don't know.
Well, he is the emperor. I'm sure he's busy.
Emperor or no emperor, it's called common courtesy.
If that were me, I'd march right back there
and demand to see him, and you know I would.
Sweetie, sweetie, think of the baby.
Pacha, I'm fine. This baby's not coming for a while,
but even if it was, I'd give that guy a piece of my mind.
Oh, yeah. I'm just a little tired from the trip.
I'm gonna go put Misty away.
Um, I'm the one in the cart. Remember?
This story's about me, not him.
Okay. You got it? All right.
We're gonna move ahead. Sorry to slow you down.
Where'd you come from, little guy?
Oh, no! Oh, no! Oh, no!
Okay, demon llama. Just take it easy.
Yeah. Who do you think you were talkin' to?
What do you mean I don't look like the emperor?
What is this, some kind of little game you country folk like to...
My face! Aah! My beautiful, beautiful face!
- Okay, okay, okay. - I'm an ugly, stinky llama!
I'm tryin' to figure that out, okay?
I can't remember. I can't remember anything.
I remember telling you that I was building my pool
where your house was, and then you got mad at me.
And you turned me into a llama!
Yes, and then you kidnapped me.
Why would I kidnap a llama?
I have no idea. You're the criminal mastermind, not me.
Hmm. You're right. That's giving you way too much credit.
Okay. I have to get back to the palace.
I'll just snap my fingers and order her to change me back.
Hey, you. No time to waste. Let's go.
Hey, tiny, I want to get out of this body. Wouldn't you? Now let's go.
You want to run that by me again?
I can't let you go back unless you change your mind
and build your summer home somewhere else.
Hmm. I got a little secret for you. Come here. No, closer.
I don't make deals with peasants!
Then I guess I can't take you back.
I can find my own way back.
It's a little dangerous if you don't know the way.
No, really. I'm telling you, there are jaguars and snakes
I'm not kidding. Listen, you cannot go in there.
If there's no Kuzco, there's no Kuzcotopia.
Oh, a leaf. Oh, it might attack me.
Please. Never find my way? I'm the emperor, and as such,
I'm born with an innate sense of direction.
Okay, that was the freakiest thing I've ever seen.
Why, I don't know what to say.
No, no. No, no, no, no.
Don't worry, Your Highness. I gotcha. You're safe now.
Maybe I'm just new to this whole rescuing thing,
might be considered kind of a step backwards,
- wouldn't you say? - No, no, no. It's okay.
I don't know about you, but I'm getting all funned out.
Don't tell me. We're about to go over a huge waterfall.
- Yep. - Sharp rocks at the bottom?
Your Highness, can you hear me?
For the last time, it was not a kiss.
And if you would've done what I ordered you to do in the first place,
we all could've been spared your little kiss of life.
But now that you're here, you will take me back to the palace.
I'll have Yzma change me back,
and then I'll start construction on Kuzcotopia.
Okay, now, look, I think we got off on the wrong foot here.
I just think if you really thought about it,
you'd decide to build your home on a different hilltop.
And why would I do that?
Deep down, I think you'll realize
that you're forcing an entire village out of their homes just for you.
What? Wait, wait. How can you be this way?
All you care about is building your summer home
and filling it with stuff for you.
Everyone else in the kingdom gets it.
You're the only one that doesn't seem to be with the program,
You know what? Someday, you're going to wind up all alone,
and you'll have no one to blame but yourself.
Thanks for that. I'll log that away.
Now, for the final time, I order you to take me back to the palace.
Looks to me like you're stuck out here,
because unless you change your mind, I'm not taking you back.
Because unless you change your mind,
Me, me, me. Moo, moo, moo.
Huh? What? I didn't do anything. I didn't...
You going to build a fire or what? What's going on?
He's never going to change his mind.
How am I ever going to get out of here?
And so, it is with great sadness
that we mourn the sudden departure of our beloved prince...
on the very eve of his 18th birthday.
His legacy will live on in our hearts...
Well, he ain't gettin' any deader.
I must admit you had me worried when you mixed up those poisons,
but now that Kuzco is dead, all is forgiven.
I mean, you can't get much deader than he... than he is right now.
Unless, of course, we killed him again.
Hey, look, the royal dresser's here.
I should tell you right now I'm kind of hard to fit.
I wear a 66 long and a 31 waist.
I need to hear these words.
Do you need to hear all those words exactly?
Well, he's not as dead as we would've hoped.
I just thought I'd give you the heads-up in case Kuzco ever came back.
Yeah. That would be kind of awkward, especially after that lovely eulogy.
You and I are going out to find him. If he talks, we are through!
I had a dream that Dad was tied to a log
and was careening out of control down a raging river of death!
All right, all right, it's okay.
- It was awful! - It's okay, it's okay. Tipo, calm down.
Your dad's fine. He just went back to see the emperor.
Like you told him to, 'cause you're always right.
Well, in my dream, Dad had to kiss a llama.
Yeah, like that would ever happen.
So, I was thinking that when I got back to the city,
I mean, there's lots of hilltops, and maybe I might, you know...
Because you know that means you're doing something nice for someone else.
No, I know that. I know.
And you're all right with that?
Don't shake unless you mean it.
Let's get you back to the palace.
Okay. Once we cross this bridge,
it's only an hour to the palace.
Good, because believe it or not, I think I need a bath.
No. I don't think I will.
You're going to leave me here?
Well, I was going to have you imprisoned for life,
but I kind of like this better.
I thought you were a changed man.
Oh, come on. I had to say something to get you to take me back to the city.
So all of it was a lie?
Uh, yeah, yeah. It all was a lie.
You know, the funny thing about shaking hands is...
Are you okay? Are you all right?
Yeah. Yeah. I think I'm all right.
That's for going back on your promise!
Yeah. That's for kidnapping me and taking me to your village...
Which I'm still gonna destroy, by the way.
Why did I risk my life for a selfish brat like you?
I was always taught that there was some good in everyone,
but, oh, you proved me wrong.
Oh, boo-hoo. Now I feel really bad. Bad llama.
I could've let you die out there in that jungle,
and then all my problems would be over.
Well, that makes you ugly and stupid.
What are we gonna do? We're gonna die!
We're gonna die! That's it for me!
I have an idea. Give me your arm.
When I say go, push against my back,
and we'll walk up the hill. Ready?
Ow! You did that on purpose.
Now, we're gonna have to work together to get out of this, so follow my lead.
Don't look down! Now, stay with me. Stay with me.
Stretch out your neck, and I'll grab the rope.
How do I know you won't let me fall after you grab the rope?
You're just gonna have to trust me!
You know, it's a good thing you're not a big, fat guy,
or this would be really difficult.
Take your time. No hurry here.
Whoo-hoo! Yeah! Ooh, look at me and my bad self.
I snatched you right out of the air.
"Ooh, I'm a crumbly canyon wall, and I'm taking you with me."
That there is some good in you after all.
- Nuh-huh. - Hey, you could've let me fall.
Come on, what's the big deal? Nobody's that heartless.
Don't read too much into it. It was a one-time thing.
With that bridge out, it's a four-day walk to the palace.
What? You mean you're still taking me back?
I shook on it, didn't I?
Well, yeah, but I hope you realize that doesn't change a thing.
I'm still building Kuzcotopia when I get back.
Well, four days is a long time.
Who knows? Maybe you'll change your mind.
What are the chances of you carrying me?
We've searched every village surrounding the palace
and still no sign of Kuzco.
- Just one more for exotic bird bingo. - Ahh!
She's not the easiest person to get close to.
There's a wall there. Trust me.
Are you talking to that squirrel?
I had to be versed in all the woodland creatures.
Hey, it doesn't always have to be about you.
This poor little guy has had it rough.
Seems a talking llama gave him a hard time the other day.
Uh, he doesn't really want to talk to you.
I hate being in the middle.
Uh, could you give us a little room here?
Now ask him which way the talking llama went!
Well, as soon as we get something to eat,
you're walking the rest of the way.
Welcome to Mudka's Meat Hut, home of the mug...
- Of meat. What'll it be? - Ahem.
We'll have two specials. Is that all right, dear?
Oh, whatever you say, pumpkin. You know what I like.
Bless you for coming out in public.
And an onion log. To split.
Ordering! I need two heartburns and a deep-fried doorstop on table 12!
Okay, so I'll admit this was a good idea.
When will you learn that all my ideas are good ones?
because I thought you going into the jungle by yourself,
lying to me to take you back to the palace
Oh, yeah, anything sounds bad when you say it with that attitude.
Hot and crispy pill bug for the happy couple.
Oh, here. Let me get that for you.
I'm just going to slip into the kitchen and have a word with the chef.
You're gonna get us thrown out.
Please. With this disguise, I'm invisible.
We've been walking around in circles for who knows how long.
That is the last time we take directions from a squirrel.
I should have done away with Kuzco myself when I had the chance.
Oh, you really gotta stop beating yourself up about that.
Uh-oh. I'll get you another one there, Yzma.
You using that fork there, pal?
- Wrestled you in high school? - Don't remember that.
Miss Narca's interpretive dance, two semesters.
I was usually in the back because of my weak ankles.
Come on, pal. You gotta help me out here.
I don't think we've ever met, but I gotta go.
Don't worry. I'll think of it.
Look, all I know is the food looked iffy.
I'm not the only one that thinks that, I'm sure.
So I'm just checking to make sure
you're going to take the main course up a notch.
Is there anything on this menu that is not swimming in gravy?
Hang on. I'll go ask the chef.
Is there or is there not anything edible
Hey, I didn't ask him about dessert yet!
Hey, pal, what's your policy on making special orders?
You want a special order, then you make it! I quit!
I try and I try, but there's no respect for anyone with vision. That's it!
There's just nothing I can do about it!
Three pork combos, extra bacon on the side,
two chili cheese samplers, a basket of liver and onion rings,
a catch of the day, and a steak cut in the shape of a trout.
Three oinkers wearing pants, plate of hot air,
basket of grandma's breakfast, and change the bull to a gill, got it.
No time to explain. We gotta get out of here.
What is he doing in there?
- Come on! - In a minute. I'm still hungry.
Okay, I'll make it simple for you.
I'll have a spinach omelet with wheat toast.
- Pickup! - Kronk! What are you doing?
- Kinda busy here. - Why am I not surprised?
Oh, well, while you're at it,
make me the special. And hold the gravy!
You know what? On second thought, make my omelet a meat pie.
Can I order the potatoes as a side dish?
- I'll have to charge you full price. - Ooh!
Hey, how about a side of potatoes, my buddy?
You got it. Want cheese on those potatoes?
Thank you, Kronk. Cheddar will be fine.
- Cheddar spuds coming up. - Spuds yes, cheese no.
- Hold the cheese. - No, I want the cheese.
Come on, make up your mind!
Excuse me. You see that woman over there?
No problem, hon. We do that all the time.
Happy, happy birthday From all of us to you
We wish it was our birthday So we could party, too
Happy, happy birthday May all your dreams come true
Look, there's two people in there looking for you.
- What? - A big guy and a skinny old woman.
Wait. Was this woman scary beyond all reason?
- Oh, yeah. - That's Yzma and Kronk! I'm saved!
Trust me, they're not here to save you.
They'll take me back to the palace.
Thanks for your help. You've been great.
I can take it from here.
You don't understand. They're trying to kill you.
Kill me? Their whole world revolves around me.
- No. I can't let you! - What? Wha...
You don't want to take me back to the palace.
You want to keep me stranded out here forever.
This has all been an act, and I almost fell for it.
Will you just listen to me...
No, no, you listen to me. All you care about is your stupid hilltop!
You don't care about me. Now, just get out of here.
- Oh, this entire mess is all your fault. - What'd I do?
If you hadn't mixed up those poisons, Kuzco would be dead now!
There'll be no more diversions until we track that llama down and kill him!
Said I was sorry. Can't just let it go.
The empire will finally be rid of that useless slug.
Nobody really seems to care that he's gone, do they?
So this is where you came in.
See, just like I said, I'm the victim here.
I didn't do anything, and they ruined my life
Hey, give it a rest up there, will you?
What? I'm just telling them what happened.
They saw the whole thing, they know what happened.
He's the peasant who I saw leaving the city
who disappeared into the crowd with Kuzco on the back of his cart.
He must have taken him back to his village,
so if we find the village, we find him, and if we find him, we find Kuzco.
Oh, yeah, it's all comin' together.
So, there we were standing on the cliff,
and the ground started to rumble.
And just as it started to go, he grabbed me before I fell.
You know, call me crazy for following this guy all the way out here,
but as much as he tries to deny it, I know there's some good in him.
Besides, I couldn't just leave him out here all alone.
I mean, a really lousy llama.
what I said to you back at the diner, that, I didn't really...
So, you tired of being a llama?
Okay, we're just gonna stop at the house and get some supplies.
- Then we'll be on our way, right? - Right.
Hey there, Pacha. You just missed your relatives.
We just sent them up to your house.
See, there was this big guy and this older woman who was...
So, remind me again how you're related to Pacha?
Why, I'm his third cousin's brother's wife's step-niece's great-aunt.
Ninety-nine monkeys jumpin' on the bed
One fell off and bumped his head
You know, I am so sorry that you had to come all this way,
but as I said to you before,
you may recall, Pacha is not here.
I'll be sure and tell him you came by.
Oh, would you, please? That would be just great.
When I give the word, we search the house.
Okay, but I still have 94 monkeys to go.
So, while we're waiting for Paca...
Perhaps we can have a tour of your lovely home.
You know, why don't you just come back when Pacha gets home?
I'm sure he'd love to show you the...
I think I left something in the oven.
This is my variation of double-dutch. On the signal, we switch places.
So, we have to get back to the palace, find the lab, and change him back.
I don't believe you're really my great-aunt.
Go. I'll stall them long enough for you two to get a head start.
You have a lovely wife. They're both very pretty.
Listen, sister, we're not leaving until...
I show you the house. Of course.
Was it a good idea to leave your family with those two?
Oh, don't worry. They can handle themselves.
What do you mean, the door is stuck? Try jiggling the handle.
There is no handle in here.
All right, I've had enough of this.
Tell us where the talking llama is and we'll burn your house to the ground.
Tell us where the talking llama is or we'll burn your house to the ground.
That seems like a pretty crucial conjunction.
That's it! Kronk, break the door down!
Break it down? Are you kidding me? This is hand-carved mahogany.
I don't care, you fool. Get out of my way.
- A-one... - Okay, kids, you know what to do.
Okay, children, on your mark, get set, go!
Ow, ow! Stop it, you little brats!
Oh, there they go, Kronk! They're getting away!
Well, I had a great time.
Let's not wait until the next family reunion to get together.
Okay, why does she even have that lever?
and keep your arms and legs in at all times.
- What does it look like? - I don't know. Just keep looking.
It has to be one of these. Lions, tigers, bears...
- How did you get back here before us? - Uh...
Well, you got me. By all accounts, it doesn't make sense.
Maybe I wasn't as nice as I should have been,
but, Yzma, do you really want to kill me?
Just think of it as you're being let go,
that your life's going in a different direction,
that your body's part of a permanent outplacement.
Hey, that's kind of like what he said to you when you got fired.
I know. It's called a cruel irony like my dependence on you.
I can't believe this is happening!
Then I bet you weren't expecting this.
Hey, you're not backing down now, are you, big guy?
Sorry I'm late. So, what'd I miss?
Well, Yzma just tossed me this knife
and asked me to, you know, take them out.
Then this guy popped up and we waited for you,
Kronk! Why did I think you could do this?
It's like I'm talking to a monkey.
A really, really big stupid monkey named Kronk!
